description: Synthetic one-page CIM-style description for a fictional infrastructure acquisition opportunity. NOT real. Hand-crafted for measurement.
user_query: Screen this opportunity. Apply the 5-test actionability gate. Identify the right-to-win angle. Recommend whether to pursue, pass, or request specific diligence to form a view.
source_content:
PROJECT MERIDIAN — CONFIDENTIAL OPPORTUNITY OVERVIEW

Transaction: Acquisition of 100% equity in MidContinent Power LLC (the 'Company'), a 1,150 MW combined-cycle gas-fired generation portfolio operating in the MISO Zone 5 footprint.

Assets: Two CCGT facilities — Site A (Indiana, 720 MW, COD 2017, Mitsubishi M501J) and Site B (Illinois, 430 MW, COD 2014, GE 7FA.05). Both interconnected to MISO at 345 kV. Combined heat rate weighted-average ~6,800 BTU/kWh. Average capacity factor over trailing 36 months: 62%.

Ownership: Currently 100% owned by an institutional credit fund acquiring through a 2018 distressed restructuring. Fund is in wind-down; this is a forced sale within the next 12 months.

Financials (LTM Q1 2026):
- Revenue: $612M
- EBITDA: $187M (margin 30.6%)
- Capacity revenue: 71% of EBITDA
- Energy + AS revenue: 29% of EBITDA
- Maintenance capex run-rate: $18M / yr

Contracts: Site A has a 10-year tolling agreement with a Midwest LSE through 2031, fixed capacity payment ~$70M / yr. Site B is fully merchant in MISO Zone 5 with bilateral hedges out 24 months covering ~60% of expected dispatch.

Market position: Site A is dispatched in the bottom quartile of the MISO supply stack — high-merit-order asset. Site B is mid-merit, dispatched ~58% of hours. Both qualify for 2027/28 PJM integration if the proposed seam reform passes (low probability per Stonepeak and ECP commentary in Q1 2026).

Ask: $1.85B enterprise value (~9.9x LTM EBITDA), seller open to up to 30% deferred consideration tied to 2027 capacity auction clearing prices.

Diligence open items: (1) Site A tolling counterparty credit profile post-Moody's downgrade in March 2026; (2) Site B air permit renewal due 2027 — incremental capex if NOx limits tighten; (3) potential PJM integration upside not in base case; (4) GenCo carve-out interest from local utilities in adjacent footprint.

Process: Sell-side advisor running a limited Round 1 with seven invited bidders. Indications due in 6 weeks. Stonepeak and ArcLight believed to be at the table; ECP declined.
